name: Local L2R Windows Server Scanner
notes: Reports a source IP address attempting reconnaissance or suspicious connections on common Windows server ports to more than 200 hosts in 20 minutes.  This can be false positive prone for busy windows servers.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Suspicious Events with the same source IP more than 5 times
conditions_text: when the event context is Local to Local
when an event matches any of the following BB:PortDefinition: Windows Ports
when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Recon Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Suspicious Events with the same source IP more than 5 times, across more than 200 destination IP within 20 minutes
thresholds: when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Recon Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Suspicious Events with the same source IP more than 5 times, across more than 200 destination IP within 20 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Local L2R Windows Server Scanner on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event context is Local to Local
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:PortDefinition: Windows Ports
AND when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Recon Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Suspicious Events with the same source IP more than 5 times, across more than 200 destination IP within 20 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Local Windows Scanner Detected

Event description: Detected  a source IP address attempting reconnaissance or suspicious connections on common Windows server ports to more than 200 hosts in 20 minutes.  This can be false positive prone for busy windows servers, but is not normal activity for windows desktops.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should not contribute to the naming of the associated offense(s)
